name: The Perse School Singapore
tagline: A Cambridge primary education from one of Britain's oldest independent schools
description: The Perse School Singapore is the first international campus of The Perse School Cambridge, a prestigious British independent school founded in 1615. Opened in Singapore in January 2025 and now situated on Chestnut Drive in Bukit Timah, the school currently caters to children aged 3 to 11 (Nursery through Year 6) with plans to expand to Year 13. The curriculum follows the Cambridge Primary programme enriched with Singapore Mathematics and daily Mandarin instruction. With a cap of 24 students per class and close ties to its Cambridge parent school, The Perse Singapore offers a quintessentially British prep-school experience within a tropical setting.

admission.process: Families submit an online enquiry and application. Nursery and Reception applicants attend a play-based observation, while Year 1 and above complete age-appropriate assessments in English and Mathematics.
admission.deadlines: Rolling admissions year-round. Main entry points are Nursery, Reception, and Year 3. Academic year starts in August.

faqs.question: What is the affiliation with The Perse School Cambridge?
faqs.answer: The Perse School Singapore is operated under direct licence from The Perse School Cambridge (UK), founded in 1615. The Singapore campus shares the ethos, curriculum framework, and quality standards of its Cambridge parent while adapting to the local context.

faqs.question: Will The Perse Singapore offer secondary education?
faqs.answer: Yes. The school has announced plans to phase in secondary education (Years 7-13), beginning with Year 7 in 2026, eventually leading to IGCSEs and A-Level examinations.

seo.title: The Perse School Singapore — British Cambridge Primary Education in Bukit Timah | EduAtlas
seo.description: The Perse School Singapore: First international campus of Cambridge UK's Perse School (est. 1615). Ages 3-11, Cambridge Primary with Singapore Maths. View 2026 fees and admissions.
